details: Beginning static numerical stability analysis of multigammaln (p=4)
ttnn/ttnn/operations/multigammaln/kernels/multigammaln_compute.cpp — main compute kernel, 252 lines, 4 inlined lgamma sub-phases + 4-way SfpuAdd sum
ttnn/ttnn/operations/multigammaln/multigammaln_program_descriptor.py — confirms HiFi4 + fp32_dest_acc_en=True, fp32 CBs, no UnpackToDestMode override (defaults)
ttnn/ttnn/operations/multigammaln/multigammaln.py — entry point, dtype-checked to fp32, no compute_kernel_config exposed
ttnn/cpp/ttnn/operations/eltwise/unary/device/kernels/compute/lgamma_kernel.cpp — reference recipe lines 34-137, mirrored 4× in multigammaln compute kernel

tt_metal/hw/ckernels/wormhole_b0/metal/llk_api/llk_sfpu/ckernel_sfpu_lgamma.h — fp32 Stirling has Taylor bridges near z=1, z=2 with 5-term polynomials; Bernoulli correction uses 4 coefficients (r0..r3); adjusted variant returns +inf when in==±inf (exp==128, man==0)
reader.cpp + writer.cpp — pure data movement, no precision concerns

No reductions, no matmul, no accumulation across tiles — operation is pure elementwise SFPU. Fidelity-sensitive ops limited to mul_binary_tile and sub_binary_tile (both FPU paths via SFPU binary helpers — actually SFPU-resident, not FPU). HiFi4 chosen primarily as defensive default, not because FPU mul is in critical path.

Identified key error sources: (1) approximate reciprocal in Stirling Bernoulli correction, (2) frac/floor of large x where x ≫ 1.0 loses precision, (3) sin_tile at pi*frac argument is SFPU-approx, (4) the reflection adjustment subtraction reflection_adj - res_stirling is a catastrophic-cancellation candidate when reflection_adj ≈ res_stirling, (5) the 4-way SfpuAdd accumulation of four lgamma terms in DEST (4 additions in fp32 dest, ≤4 terms — negligible).

Documented gap: log_tile<false>(1) (precise log) is used for the z=log(z) feeding Stirling, but the second log_tile() at line 167 (default approx) is used for log|sin|. Mixed mode is intentional and matches lgamma_kernel.cpp reference.
